CLAUDE.md); hook architecture gap — prefer-ripgrep hook intercepts argv[0] only, mid-pipeline grep after && or ; is structurally invisible; fifth or more consecutive cycle of RECURRING with zero improvement

cat-instead-of-read:NEW_NAMED_SUBVARIANT — two units (6ec6743c-22, 6ec6743c-23) use cat to read existing files rather than the Read tool; distinct from the previously tracked cat-heredoc-file-write subvariant (which writes files via heredoc instead of Write tool); both represent the same shell-first reflex but in the reading direction; 6ec6743c-23 is the cleaner signal: cat /tmp/task2-modal-review.md executed immediately after the agent just wrote that file, a context where Read tool is clearly correct

ls-t-for-timestamp-sort is now a stable subvariant appearing across 6+ units in 2 sessions; the LS/Glob tools have no timestamp-sort mode making this pattern partially justified; consider adding a narrow carve-out to shell preference guidance explicitly noting that newest-file-by-mtime discovery via ls -t | head -1 is acceptable while pure directory listing ls violations remain blocked — this would reduce false-positive tracking noise and focus remediation on the actual violations

cat-instead-of-read should be formally named and tracked as a subvariant within shell_tool_preference_violations; the pattern is now appearing independently of cat-heredoc-file-write and needs its own label for accurate recurrence measurement

scope_expansion S3 atone was filed correctly with RCA and precheck procedure in unit 6ec6743c-17; the procedure quality appears adequate; the precheck ('grep for an existing derivation AND name the caller right now') is specific and runnable; success depends on the precheck being applied before the abstraction is authored, not after
